highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.118 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds vim visual mode (v and V) with full selection and operator support, making keyboard-driven editing much more powerful for vim users. It also consolidates /cost and /stats into a single /usage command, and introduces named custom themes you can create and switch between via /theme. Update now to take advantage of cleaner command navigation and improved terminal customization.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.116 Released
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.116 brings up to 67% faster /resume performance on large sessions (40MB+), which is a significant quality-of-life improvement if you work on long-running projects. MCP startup is also faster when multiple stdio servers are configured, with resource template loading now deferred until you actually use an @-mention. If you rely heavily on MCP servers or resume large sessions frequently, this update is worth grabbing right away.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.113 Released
highlights.summary: This release switches the CLI to spawn a native Claude Code binary via platform-specific optional dependencies instead of bundled JavaScript, which can improve startup performance and reduce overhead. It also adds a sandbox.network.deniedDomains setting so you can block specific domains even when a broader wildcard allowedDomains rule would otherwise permit them. Developers who manage network sandboxing policies or use Claude Code in restricted environments should review the new network controls.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family. As the most capable tier of Claude models, this release is directly relevant to Claude Code users who want to leverage the most powerful reasoning and coding capabilities available. Keep an eye on the Anthropic blog for details on how to select Opus 4.7 within Claude Code via the /model command.

highlights.title: SPICE Simulation → Oscilloscope → Verification with Claude Code (HN Show)
highlights.summary: A developer built MCP servers for both an oscilloscope and a SPICE simulator, allowing Claude Code to automatically close the feedback loop between hardware simulation and real-world measurements. This is a compelling real-world example of using Claude Code's MCP integration to automate complex engineering workflows that would otherwise require manual back-and-forth. If you work in electronics or embedded systems, this project shows how MCP servers can connect Claude Code to physical hardware and domain-specific tools.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a focused, time-efficient walkthrough of Claude Code aimed at developers who want to get productive quickly without spending hours on documentation. At 30 minutes, it's a great starting point for understanding the core workflow, key commands, and best practices before diving into more advanced features. Bookmark this as your go-to resource to share with teammates who are just getting started with Claude Code.

highlights.title: Understanding Anthropic: Models, Funding, and Vision (Bilibili — Chinese)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili video (in Chinese) provides a broad overview of Anthropic as a company, covering its top-tier models including Claude Code, its massive funding rounds, and the CEO's controversial public statements. For developers curious about the company behind the tools they use, it gives useful context on Anthropic's strategic focus and business trajectory. The video concludes that Anthropic is a uniquely focused AI safety company that is simultaneously building toward highly autonomous AI systems.

highlights.title: Claude Code Possibly Being Removed from Anthropic Pro Plan
highlights.summary: A discussion on Hacker News is circulating around reports that Anthropic may be reconsidering or restricting Claude Code access under its Pro subscription tier. If you currently rely on the Pro plan for Claude Code usage, it's worth monitoring official Anthropic channels for any announcements about plan changes. This is still unconfirmed speculation, but it's a good reminder to check your subscription terms and consider how a change could affect your development workflow.

tips_of_the_day: Use /usage (the new unified command in v2.1.118) at the end of each session to review your token consumption and cost trends — building this habit early helps you optimize prompts before bills get large.

If your Claude Code sessions are slow to resume, upgrade to v2.1.116 immediately; for sessions over 40MB you can expect up to 67% faster load times, and you can check session size by looking at your ~/.claude/projects directory.

When connecting multiple MCP stdio servers, avoid adding resource-heavy servers you only use occasionally — with v2.1.116, resource templates are deferred until you type an @-mention, so lean into that lazy-loading behavior to keep startup times snappy.
